Parties & Contacts

Merlin uses a party as the shared identity record for any organisation or person that the business interacts with. Suppliers and clients are both parties; so are the individual contact people attached to them. This page describes the data model used across the Core module. Developers working on supplier or client features should read this before touching the relevant code.

The Party model

The Party model is the parent in a class table inheritance scheme. It holds the shared primary key (a UUID) and the fields that apply to any kind of party regardless of type. The practical display name for a party is always accessed via $party->displayName, which returns the trading name if set, otherwise the legal name.

You never create a Party directly. You create a Person or a Business (see below), and the party row is created automatically as the shared parent.

Persons versus Businesses

Person and Business are the two concrete party subtypes. Both share the Party primary key — the persons and businesses tables have a party_id foreign key that is also their primary key, pointing back to the parties table.

From a Party instance you can access the subtype via $party->person or $party->business. If a party is a business, $party->person will be null, and vice versa. Business types (e.g. sole trader, company, trust) are defined in config/party.php.

Party relationships

A party becomes a supplier or a client through a PartyRelationship record. A party can have both a supplier relationship and a client relationship simultaneously — these are separate rows. The relationship_type column on party_relationships holds the string 'supplier' or 'client'.

Relationship metadata

Per-relationship-type fields are stored in a single metadata JSON column on party_relationships, not in separate typed columns. This keeps the table stable as new relationship types are introduced.

Current metadata shapes:

Relationship typeMetadata keys
supplierdefault_payable_account_id (UUID or null), payment_term_id (UUID or null)
clientdefault_receivable_account_id (UUID or null), payment_term_id (UUID or null)

PartyRelationship exposes these as getter-only accessor properties so callers can read $rel->default_payable_account_id as a normal property. For writing, always use $rel->mergeMetadata([...]). Do not assign to the accessor properties directly — Eloquent attribute setters that return an array bypass the JSON cast encoder and leave a raw PHP array in the attributes, breaking subsequent JSON decoding on the same request.

Contact assignments

People can be attached to a party as named contacts through the ContactAssignment model. A contact assignment links a Person to a Party with a role (e.g. accounts contact, primary contact). Contact roles are defined in config/party.php. This is used when selecting which contacts receive sales invoice emails.

Addresses

The Address model is polymorphic and can be attached to any party. Address types (e.g. postal, physical, billing) are defined in config/party.php. A party can have multiple addresses of different types.
